title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A municipal incident-response team and public client developers. They need an outcome-driven Go service with explicit lifecycle, HTTP semantics, conflict-free ServeMux routes, composed handlers, trusted host and proxy rules, redacted evidence, and accessible API behavior. Planned artifact: Deliver Routed and Bounded Civic Service Core with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
Test method semantics against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, and changed resource state.
Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Project Service Outcome Contract
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 6499 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive effective authority only through an explicit trusted-proxy boundary; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Request Identity Redaction, keep Request Identity Redaction connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.

hints: For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Request Identity Redaction, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Request Identity Redaction precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Request Identity Redaction, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A project team handles case 6508 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive effective authority only through an explicit trusted-proxy boundary; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.

For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Request Identity Redaction, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Logging RequestURI is safe because authorization values appear only in headers.
